Title: The ZmMYB84-ZmPKSB regulatory module controls male fertility through modulating anther cuticle—pollen exine trade-off in maize anthers

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: sporopollenin precursors; anther cutin and wax
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylpropanoid-derived sporopollenin biosynthesis linked to anther cuticle lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: sporopollenin precursor biosynthesis in tapetum; interface with cutin/wax branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Anther-specific, detected from stages S8b to S9-10, peak at S9
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Anther-specific, detected from stages S8b to S9-10, peak at S9
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; EMS mutant analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; allelism test; qPCR; subcellular localization; recombinant enzyme assay; UPLC-MS/MS; SEM/TEM; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPKSB is the causal gene of the maize male-sterility mutant pksb. 2. It encodes an ER-localized type III polyketide synthase that uses malonyl-CoA plus C16:0- or C18:0-fatty acyl-CoA to produce triketide and tetraketide α-pyrone. 3. Loss of ZmPKSB causes thinner pollen exine but denser anther cuticle, delayed tapetal PCD, and complete male sterility, indicating it controls lipid allocation between sporopollenin and cuticle formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point/balance controller for lipid allocation between pollen exine and anther cuticle
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (previous maize anther datasets from B73, M6007, Oh43, W23; accession NA); GC-MS metabolite profiling; UPLC-MS/MS enzyme product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normal anther developmental progression; developmental stages S8b-S13
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA with C16:0- or C18:0-fatty acyl-CoA starter substrates to generate triketide and tetraketide α-pyrone products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: maize anther pollen exine and anther cuticle
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms sporopollenin precursor products and controls precursor allocation away from or toward cutin/wax accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering or selecting male sterility and controlling pollen wall/cuticle traits in maize hybrid breeding; editing should be used cautiously because loss of function causes complete male sterility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pollen exine formation and restrains excessive anther cuticle accumulation; loss of function causes thinner exine, denser cuticle, delayed tapetal degeneration, and male sterility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic triad C171, H320, and N353 is essential for enzyme activity; mutation likely increases upstream fatty acyl-CoA pools that are redirected to cutin/wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete male sterility upon loss of function; altered lipid partitioning between exine and cuticle
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sporopollenin, cutin and wax are crucial for pollen wall integrity, anther protection, pollen-stigma interaction, and successful fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: type III polyketide synthase superfamily; CHS_N domain; CHS_C domain; catalytic triad C171/H320/N353

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: sporopollenin precursors; anther cutin and wax
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis and allocation balance of sporopollenin versus cutin/wax during anther development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator of ZmPKSB and major regulator required for proper pollen exine formation while preventing excessive anther cuticle accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ZmPKSB; ZmMs25; nine sporopollenin-related genes including ZmACOS5, ZmIPE1, ZmMs45, ZmMs25, ZmDFR1, ZmDFR2, ZmPKSA-1, ZmPKSA-2, ZmABCG26; cutin/wax-related genes such as ZmHCT12, ZmLTL1, ZmABCG11, ZmCER8, ZmCER1, ZmGL2, ZmCL3929, ZmKCAS-4, ZmGL8
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sporopollenin, cutin and wax are essential for pollen wall integrity, anther surface protection, pollen-stigma interaction, and fertility
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates ZmPKSB; activates sporopollenin-related genes; partly modulates cutin/wax-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression overlaps with ZmPKSB from stages S8a-S10 with common peak at S9; myb84 mutant phenocopies dense anther cuticle and thin exine of pksb
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: maize anther pollen exine and anther cuticle
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq coexpression analysis; qPCR; mutant analysis; transient dual-luciferase reporter assay; EMSA; SEM; cutin/wax quantification
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Anther-expressed from stages S8a to S10 with peak at S9
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Anther-expressed from stages S8a to S10 with peak at S9
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB84 expression overlaps with ZmPKSB during anther development and is required for ZmPKSB transcription. 2. ZmMYB84 directly binds and activates the ZmPKSB promoter. 3. Loss of ZmMYB84 causes thinner pollen exine, denser anther cuticle, and increased cutin/wax content, establishing the ZmMYB84-ZmPKSB module as a controller of lipid trade-off in maize anthers.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator target to manipulate male fertility and anther lipid traits in maize; not suitable for simple knockout if fertility must be maintained
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes male sterility and abnormal lipid partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normal anther developmental progression; developmental stages S6-S13
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates the lipid-metabolic program needed for normal pollen exine formation and balanced anther cuticle deposition; loss of function leads to dense cuticle, thin exine, and male sterility
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (previous maize anther datasets from B73, M6007, Oh43, W23; accession NA)
